text: Dijkstra's algorithm computes shortest paths in graphs with nonnegative edge weights.
A priority queue is commonly used to repeatedly select the unsettled vertex with minimum tentative distance.
Breadth first search explores an unweighted graph layer by layer from the source vertex.
The budgeted maximum coverage problem selects sets with costs to maximize covered elements under a budget.
Monotone submodular maximization under a knapsack constraint can be approximately solved by greedy methods.
Maximal marginal relevance reranks documents by trading off query relevance against redundancy and novelty.
Retrieval augmented generation inserts selected external passages into a language model context window.
Dense embeddings can represent semantic similarity but may add nondeterministic dependencies to a small experiment.
